It consists of a senior group of opposition spokespeople who, under the leadership of the Leader of the Opposition, form an alternative cabinet to that of the government, and whose members shadow or mirror the positions of each individual member of the Cabinet. It is the Shadow Cabinet's responsibility to scrutinize the policies and actions of the government, as well to offer an alternative program.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : PARLIAMENTARY SYSTEM OF GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: Which of the following made the earliest contact with the Nigerian society?
explanation: The Portuguese made the earliest contact with the Nigerian society. They arrived in Nigeria in the late 15th century during the Age of Discovery. The Portuguese explorer, João de Santarém, discovered the Lagos lagoon in 1472. The Portuguese were the first Europeans to engage in trade with the peoples of what is now Nigeria, and they established a number of trading posts on the coast.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : COMPARISON OF BRITISH AND FRENCH COLONIAL ADMINISTRATION

subject: Government
q: The adoption of a federal system of government in Nigeria under colonial rule was necessitated mainly by
explanation: The adoption of a federal system of government in Nigeria under colonial rule was necessitated mainly by cultural diversity. Nigeria is a country with over 250 ethnic groups, each with its unique language, customs, and traditions. This diversity made it difficult for a unitary system of government to effectively manage the affairs of the country. Therefore, a federal system of government, which allows for power to be shared among the central government and the states or regions, was adopted to accommodate this diversity.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM

subject: Government
q: Public corporations are established mainly to
explanation: Public corporations are established mainly to provide services at subsidized rates. Public corporations, also known as public sector organizations, are entities that are owned and operated by the government. They are established to provide essential services to the public at affordable rates. These services may include utilities like water and electricity, transportation, healthcare, and education. The aim is not to make profit but to ensure that all citizens have access to these basic services.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: Indirect rule as a British colonial policy was first introduced in
explanation: India saw forms of indirect rule much earlier, particularly after the British East India Company began gaining control in the 18th century.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: Following the 1976 Local Government Reform is Nigeria, the local governments were empowered to
explanation: The 1976 Local Government Reform in Nigeria was a major restructuring of the local government system in the country. One of the key changes was the empowerment of local governments to promulgate and enforce bye-laws. This gave them a degree of autonomy and allowed them to manage local affairs more effectively. Bye-laws are laws that are made by a local authority and apply only within their specific geographical area.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT :FEATURES OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT REFORMS 1976, 1989 - STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS, FINANCE AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS

subject: Government
q: In the Nigerian Constitution, the powers to create local governments lie with the
explanation: In the Nigerian Constitution, the power to create local governments lies with the State Houses of Assembly. This is because the constitution gives the state the power to create, merge, or alter the boundaries of local governments as they deem fit.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: The pre-colonial Igbo political system was largely
explanation: The pre-colonial Igbo political system was largely republican. This means that it was a system where the head of state is an elected or nominated president rather than a monarch. The Igbo political system was decentralized and democratic in nature, with decisions made by a general assembly of all adult males.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following states were created in 1996?
explanation: The states of Gombe and Zamfara were created in 1996 by the military regime of General Sani Abacha. Akwa Ibom and Kogi were created in 1987 and 1991 respectively. Adamawa and Ekiti were created in 1991 and 1996 respectively. Ebonyi and Osun were created in 1996 and 1991 respectively.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM : CREATION OF STATES – 1963, 1967, 1976, 1987, 1991, 1996

subject: Government
q: Acculturation in colonial administration was associated with the
explanation: Acculturation in colonial administration was associated with the French policy of assimilation. This policy aimed at making the colonized people adopt the French culture, language, and way of life. The British policy of direct rule and indirect rule system, on the other hand, allowed the colonized people to maintain their culture and traditions under the supervision of British officials. The French policy of association was a modification of the policy of assimilation, allowing for some degree of cultural diversity.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE FRENCH COLONIAL ADMINISTRATIVE POLICY – ASSIMILATION AND ASSOCIATION

subject: Government
q: Military disengagement from politics is always preceded by
explanation: Military disengagement from politics is usually preceded by the making of a new constitution. This is because a new constitution often outlines the structure and function of the government, including the role of the military, which is typically to defend the country rather than to engage in politics.

topics.title: THE MILITARY IN NIGERIAN POLITICS : PROCESSES OF MILITARY DISENGAGEMENT

subject: Government
q: The principle of federal character was first enunciated in the
explanation: The principle of federal character was first enunciated in the 1979 Constitution of Nigeria. This principle was introduced to ensure that appointments to public service institutions fairly reflect the linguistic, ethnic, religious, and geographic diversity of the country.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM : RATIONALE FOR A FEDERAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: The final court of appeal in Nigeria during the colonial era was the
explanation: During the colonial era in Nigeria, the final court of appeal was the Privy Council. The Privy Council is a body of advisors to the British monarch. Its judicial committee serves as the highest court of appeal for several independent Commonwealth countries, the UK overseas territories, and the British Crown dependencies.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: The Native Authority system of local Administration was introduced by the British in Nigeria to
explanation: The Native Authority system of local Administration was introduced by the British in Nigeria primarily to facilitate tax collection. This system allowed the British to indirectly rule and control the local population through traditional rulers, who were given the responsibility of tax collection in their respective regions.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT :FEATURES OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT REFORMS 1976, 1989 - STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS, FINANCE AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS

subject: Government
q: The major objecitive of the manifesto of the People's Redemption Party in the Second Republic was
explanation: The People's Redemption Party (PRP) was a major political party in Nigeria during the Second Republic (1979-1983). The party was markedly leftist and its main objective was the emancipation of the masses. This means freeing the common people in a society from political, social, or economic restrictions or discrimination.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : SECOND REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The privatization of public corporations in Nigeria is undertaken by the
explanation: The privatization of public corporations in Nigeria is undertaken by the Bureau of Public Enterprises (BPE). The BPE is a Nigerian governmental agency tasked with the responsibility of implementing the policy of the Federal Government to divest its interest in parastatals, agencies, and other government businesses.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS : DEREGULATION, PRIVATIZATION, COMMERCIALIZATION – OBJECTIVES, FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: The main goal of NNDP was to
explanation: The main goal of the Nigerian National Development Plan (NNDP) was to promote economic stability and development in Nigeria.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FIRST REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The Structural Adjustment Programme was introduced by the
explanation: The Structural Adjustment Programme (SAP) was introduced by the Babangida regime in Nigeria. The SAP was an economic programme that sought to alter and restructure the Nigerian economy. It was introduced in 1986 by the then military president, General Ibrahim Babangida, due to the economic hardship experienced in the country.

topics.title: ECONOMIC POLICIES AND DEVELOPMENT STRATEGIES : STRUCTURAL ADJUSTMENT PROGRAMME (SAP)

subject: Government
q: The Ogboni cult in the pre-colonial Yoruba political system played the role of the
explanation: The Ogboni cult in the pre-colonial Yoruba political system played the role of the judiciary. They were responsible for the administration of justice and the interpretation of laws.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS : YORUBA PRE-COLONIAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: The head of the electoral body that conducted the June 12, 1993 elections was
explanation: The head of the electoral body that conducted the June 12, 1993 elections was Humphrey Nwosu. He was the chairman of the National Electoral Commission (NEC) at the time.

topics.title: ELECTORAL PROCESS

subject: Government
q: Deregulation as an economic policy borders on
options: the removal of subsidies on public utilities
the removal of restrictions and extension of competition
explanation: Deregulation as an economic policy refers to the reduction or elimination of government power in a particular industry, usually enacted to create more competition within the industry. It involves the removal of restrictions and extension of competition, not necessarily the removal of subsidies, privatization or commercialization.

topics.title: ECONOMIC POLICY

subject: Government
q: Ethnic politics in Nigeria can be traced to the
explanation: Ethnic politics in Nigeria can be traced back to the Macpherson Constitution of 1951. This constitution introduced regionalism and allowed each region to develop at its own pace, thereby creating a sense of ethnic consciousness and competition among the different ethnic groups in Nigeria.

topics.title: ETHNIC POLITICS

subject: Government
q: A member of the Commonwealth that was not colonized by Britain is
explanation: Mozambique is a member of the Commonwealth of Nations, but it was not colonized by Britain. Instead, it was colonized by Portugal. The Commonwealth of Nations is a political association of 54 member states, nearly all of which are former territories of the British Empire.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : COMMONWEALTH

subject: Government
q: The Organization of Petroleum Exporting countries came into existence at a conference held in
explanation: The Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) was created at the Baghdad Conference on September 10–14, 1960, by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and Venezuela. The five founding members were later joined by other members. OPEC's objective is to co-ordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries, in order to secure fair and stable prices for petroleum producers.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : OPEC

subject: Government
q: ECOWAS main achievement in recent years has been in the area of
explanation: The political crises in Guinea and Mali occurred in 2021. In Guinea, a coup took place in September 2021, while Mali experienced a coup in May 2021, following an earlier one in August 2020. Both events prompted significant involvement from ECOWAS in mediation and restoration of constitutional order.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : ECOWAS

subject: Government
q: In which of the following countries has the UN carried out peacekeeping operations?
explanation: The United Nations (UN) has carried out peacekeeping operations in several countries around the world. Among these are Cyprus, Somalia, and Yugoslavia. The UN's peacekeeping mission in Cyprus, known as UNFICYP, was established in 1964 to prevent further fighting between the Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot communities. In Somalia, the UN's mission, known as UNOSOM, was established in the early 1990s to address the civil war and humanitarian crisis. In Yugoslavia, the UN established UNPROFOR in the early 1990s to address the Yugoslav Wars. The UN has not carried out peacekeeping operations in Singapore, South Africa, Mexico, Nigeria, or Botswana.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : UNO

subject: Government
q: Which of the following was the main organ of the defunct OAU??
options: The Commission for Mediation, Conciliation and Arbitration
The Assembly of Heads of State and Government
explanation: The main organ of the defunct Organization of African Unity (OAU) was the Assembly of Heads of State and Government. This assembly was the supreme organ of the OAU and it was composed of heads of state and government from all member states. It was responsible for determining the OAU's policies, goals, and objectives.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : OAU/AU

subject: Government
q: A key characteristic feature of the civil service is
explanation: A key characteristic feature of the civil service is the merit system. The merit system is a system of employment where selection and promotion are based on individual ability or achievement. It is designed to ensure fairness and eliminate patronage, nepotism, and bias in the hiring and promotion process. It is a fundamental principle of public sector employment and is contrasted with the quota system, which is often seen as a form of affirmative action.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: Which of the following is a perverted form of democracy?
explanation: A perverted form of democracy is Oligarchy. In an oligarchy, power effectively rests with a small number of people. These people might be distinguished by royalty, wealth, family ties, education, corporate, religious, or military control. Such states are often controlled by families who pass their influence from one generation to the next, but inheritance is not a necessary condition for the application of this term.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : FEUDALISM (FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS)

subject: Government
q: An example of a state with an unwritten constitution is
explanation: Britain is an example of a state with an unwritten constitution. An unwritten constitution is a type of constitution where the fundamental rules often take the form of customs, usage, precedent and a variety of statutes and legal instruments. An understanding of the constitution is obtained through reading commentary by the judiciary, government committees, and legal experts.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: A characteristic of a fascist state is the existence of a
explanation: A characteristic of a fascist state is the existence of a one-party system. Fascism is a form of far-right, authoritarian political ideology that centralizes power around a single party or leader. It suppresses opposition and criticism, and often uses a policy of belligerent nationalism and racism.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : FASCISM

subject: Government
q: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is
explanation: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is suffrage. Suffrage, also known as the right to vote, is the legal right to participate in a nation's democratic process. It is a fundamental aspect of representative democracies that allows citizens to choose their representatives in government.

topics.title: THE ELECTORAL PROCESS : SUFFRAGE

subject: Government
q: Membership of a society is
explanation: Membership of a society is voluntary. This means that individuals choose to join or leave a society based on their own free will. It is not reserved for certain individuals, mandatory for everyone, or honorary.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Government
q: "Equally before the law" is a component of
explanation: "Equally before the law" is a component of the rule of law. The rule of law is a principle that all people and institutions are subject to and accountable to law that is fairly applied and enforced. It means that no one is above the law, and everyone is equal before it. This principle is not directly related to separation of powers, checks and balances, or constitutional law, although these concepts can support the rule of law.

topics.title: PRINCIPLES OF DEMOCRATIC GOVERNMENT : CONSTITUTIONALISM, RULE OF LAW AND REPRESENTATIVE GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: An example of promotional pressure group is the
explanation: A promotional pressure group is a type of pressure group that seeks to promote a particular cause, and they are often non-profit organizations. The Manufacturers Association of Nigeria is an example of a promotional pressure group as it seeks to promote the interests of manufacturers in Nigeria.

topics.title: PRESSURE GROUPS

subject: Government
q: An example of a country that operates a bicameral legislature is
explanation: A bicameral legislature is a legislative body that is divided into two separate assemblies, chambers, or houses, known as a 'bicameral system'. Nigeria operates a bicameral legislature, which consists of the House of Representatives (the lower house) and the Senate (the upper house).

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the
explanation: A political party is an institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal. This is because political parties aggregate interests and demands from various groups and individuals, and then formulate these into policy proposals that can be implemented if the party gains power.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: Government exercises political power on behalf of the
explanation: Government exercises political power on behalf of the citizens. This is because the government is elected by the citizens and is responsible for making and implementing laws and policies that are in the best interest of the citizens. The government is accountable to the citizens and is expected to act in their best interest.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: A major characteristic of a confederal state is that
options: the citizens often owe allegiance to the centre
explanation: A confederal state is a union of states where the central authority is created by the member states and is dependent on them for its authority. The supremacy of the constitution is a major characteristic of a confederal state because the constitution is the document that outlines the powers and limitations of the central authority and the member states. The constitution is supreme because it is the highest law of the land and all other laws and actions are subject to it.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE : CONFEDERAL FEATURES, REASONS FOR ADOPTION, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: The citizenship of a country can be acquired by an alien through
explanation: An alien can acquire the citizenship of a country through naturalization. Naturalization is a legal process where a non-citizen in a country may acquire citizenship or nationality of that country. It may include the fulfillment of certain conditions such as residing in that country for a specified period, demonstrating good moral character, understanding of the country's language and culture, and taking an oath of allegiance.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Government
q: An advantage of delegated legislation is
explanation: Delegated legislation refers to laws made by an individual or body under powers given to them by an Act of Parliament. The advantage of delegated legislation is its convenience. It allows the government to make changes to a law without needing to push through a completely new Act of Parliament. The whole legislative process can be time-consuming and would be even more so if every minor change in law required a completely new Act of Parliament.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION : DELEGATED LEGISLATION

subject: Government
q: A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called
explanation: A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called democracy. Democracy is a system of government where the citizens exercise power by voting. In a direct democracy, the citizens as a whole form a governing body and vote directly on each issue. In a representative democracy the citizens elect representatives from among themselves.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : DEMOCRACY FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: In the presidential system of government, the leader is basically the head of the state and
explanation: In a presidential system of government, the leader is both the head of state and head of government. This means the leader is responsible for implementing and enforcing laws written by the legislature. Therefore, the correct answer is 'government'.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : PRESIDENTIAL SYSTEM OF GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: An economic system that encourages even distribution of wealth in the society is
explanation: Socialism is an economic system where wealth and power are shared equally among the citizens. This system encourages even distribution of wealth in the society. Therefore, the correct answer is 'socialism'.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : SOCIALISM

subject: Government
q: An attribute that makes a state to be recognized as independent is
explanation: Sovereignty is the attribute that makes a state to be recognized as independent. Sovereignty refers to the full right and power of a governing body over itself, without any interference from outside sources or bodies. In political theory, sovereignty is a substantive term designating supreme legitimate authority over some polity. It is a basic principle underlying the dominant Westphalian model of state foundation.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: The act of denying an eligible person the right to vote in an election is
explanation: The act of denying an eligible person the right to vote in an election is disenfranchisement. Disenfranchisement is the revocation of the right of suffrage (the right to vote) of a person or group of people, or through practices, prevention of a person exercising the right to vote. Disenfranchisement is also termed to the revocation of power or control of a particular individual, community or being to the natural amenity they are abound in; that is to deprive of a franchise, of a legal right, of some privilege or immunity.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

subject: Government
q: In the monarchical system of government of government, sovereignty is vested in the
explanation: In a monarchical system of government, sovereignty is vested in the royalty. This means that the monarch (king, queen, emperor, etc.) has the supreme power and authority in the state. The people, legislature, and military do not hold sovereignty in a monarchy.

topics.title: SYSTEMS OF GOVERNANCE : MONARCHICAL SYSTEM OF GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: One merit of a written constitution is its
explanation: One merit of a written constitution is its ease of reference. A written constitution is a formal document defining the nature of the constitutional settlement, the rules that govern the political system and the rights of citizens and governments in a codified form. This makes it easy to refer to when needed. It is not totally flexible as it requires a special process to amend it, and its rigidity can be a disadvantage as it may not easily adapt to changing circumstances.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: A human community that is usually cohesive and homogenous is a
explanation: A nation is a human community that is usually cohesive and homogenous. This is because a nation is a large body of people united by common descent, history, culture, or language, inhabiting a particular country or territory. This makes them cohesive and homogenous. The other options do not fit the definition as accurately. A state is a political entity, a kinship is a family relationship, and a clan is a group of close-knit and interrelated families.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : SOCIETY, STATE, NATION AND NATION-STATE

subject: Government
q: In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs
explanation: In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs legislative functions. This is because the executive branch is responsible for the administration of the state bureaucracy, which can involve creating regulations or procedures, which is a form of legislation. Judicial function refers to the interpretation of laws, bureaucratic function refers to the administration of public policy, and oversight function refers to the review, monitoring, and supervision of government and public agencies.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria?
options: her location in the West African Sub-region
Her physical size, population and resource endowments
Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions
explanation: The Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria is justified by her physical size, population and resource endowments. This is because these factors give Nigeria a significant influence and role in Africa. The Afro-centric foreign policy is a policy that prioritizes the interests of Africa and its people. Nigeria, being the most populous country in Africa and rich in resources, has the capacity to influence decisions and policies in the continent.

topics.title: NIGERIA'S FOREIGN POLICY

subject: Government
q: A virile foreign policy is one characterized by
options: a vibrant press, vibrant public opinion and a dynamic civil society
a stable polity, buoyant economy and visionary leadership
the nature and dynamics of the international system
explanation: A virile foreign policy is characterized by a stable polity, buoyant economy and visionary leadership. A stable polity ensures that the country's foreign policy is consistent and not easily swayed by internal conflicts or instability. A buoyant economy provides the resources needed to implement the foreign policy. Visionary leadership ensures that the foreign policy is well directed and serves the best interests of the country.

topics.title: NIGERIA'S FOREIGN POLICY

subject: Government
q: Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference which eventually led to the independence of
explanation: Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference in 1979 which eventually led to the independence of Zimbabwe. The conference was a significant event in the history of Zimbabwe as it marked the end of white minority rule and the establishment of an independent Zimbabwe.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : COMMONWEALTH

subject: Government
q: Multi-laterism in Nigeria's foreign policy entails her
options: stand on Africa as the centrepiece of her policy
quest for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council
explanation: Multi-lateralism in Nigeria's foreign policy entails her membership of international organizations. This is because multilateralism refers to the process of organizing relations between groups of three or more states. Beyond that, it is a method of coordinating relations between nations on the basis of 'generalized principles of conduct'. This is often in the form of membership in international organizations like the United Nations, African Union, etc.

topics.title: NIGERIA’S FOREIGN POLICY : NIGERIA’S NON-ALIGNMENT POLICY

subject: Government
q: The first military coup in Nigeria toppled the government of
explanation: The first military coup in Nigeria took place on January 15, 1966, and it was led by Major Chukwuma Kaduna Nzeogwu and Emmanuel Ifeajuna. This coup ended the First Republic and marked the beginning of a series of military regimes in Nigeria. The government that was toppled was that of Alhaji Abubakar Tafawa Balewa.

topics.title: THE MILITARY IN NIGERIAN POLITICS

subject: Government
q: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for
explanation: The Supreme Court is the highest court in the land and it has the jurisdiction to adjudicate on matters between the federal and regional governments. Therefore, during the First Republic, conflicts between the federal and regional governments were reserved for the Supreme Court.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: The officer in charge of the administration of the palace in the Yoruba political structure is the
explanation: In the Yoruba political structure, the officer in charge of the administration of the palace is the Oluwo. The Oluwo is a high-ranking official in the Yoruba kingdom who is responsible for the administration and maintenance of the palace. The Ba'ale is a village head, the Ilari are the king's messengers, and the Iwarefa are the senior members of the Ogboni society.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT :FEATURES OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT REFORMS 1976, 1989 - STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS, FINANCE AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS

subject: Government
q: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of
explanation: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in the western region of Nigeria was based on the three-tier structure of native authority, division, and district councils. This system was introduced by the British colonial administration to facilitate local governance. The native authority was the highest level of local government, followed by the division and district councils.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT :FEATURES OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT REFORMS 1976, 1989 - STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS, FINANCE AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS

subject: Government
q: A major shortcoming of the Lyttleton Constitution was that
options: it did not provide for a second chamber at the federal level
the Governor-General continued to head the government
no provision was made for the office of the prime minister
it failed to provide for the office of a premier
explanation: The Lyttleton Constitution of 1954 in Nigeria did not remove the Governor-General as the head of government. The Governor-General was a representative of the British Monarch, and his position as the head of government meant that Nigeria was not fully autonomous. This was a major shortcoming of the Lyttleton Constitution.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : OLIVER LYTTLETON CONSTITUTION 1954

subject: Government
q: The option A-4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the
explanation: The Option A-4 voting system was introduced during the Third Republic in Nigeria. This system required that a candidate must first be voted for by the majority of his party members at the local level before he could proceed to the state and national levels. This was done to ensure that only popular and acceptable candidates would emerge for elections.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FOURTH REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: In orientation, early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially
explanation: Early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially reformist. This is because the early nationalists sought to reform the existing colonial system rather than completely overthrow it. They aimed to improve the conditions of Nigerians within the framework of colonial rule, advocating for more representation and better treatment. This is different from being revolutionary or radical, which would involve seeking a complete overhaul of the system, or reactionary, which would involve resisting change.

topics.title: NIGERIA IN INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : THE AFRICAN UNION

subject: Government
q: Which of the following in the Sokoto Caliphate performed functions similar to that of the Bashorun in Oyo Kingdom?
explanation: In the Sokoto Caliphate, the Waziri performed functions similar to that of the Bashorun in the Oyo Kingdom. Both the Waziri and the Bashorun were high-ranking officials who advised the ruler and played significant roles in the administration of the state. The Waziri was the vizier or prime minister in the Sokoto Caliphate, while the Bashorun was the prime minister in the Oyo Kingdom.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS : HAUSA PRE-COLONIAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: Problems arising from population census in Nigeria are mainly caused by
explanation: The problems arising from population census in Nigeria are mainly caused by political manipulation by the elite. This is because the census figures are often inflated or deflated for political reasons such as to gain more representation in the government or to receive more allocation of resources. This manipulation leads to unreliable census figures, which can affect planning and development.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Government
q: Which of the following formalized colonial penetration into Africa?
explanation: The Berlin Conference formalized colonial penetration into Africa. The conference, which took place from 1884 to 1885, was organized by European powers to establish rules for the colonization of Africa. It led to the 'Scramble for Africa', where European powers divided Africa into territories for colonization. The Paris Treaty, Treaty of Wesphalia, and Treaty of Versailles were significant in other historical contexts, but not directly related to the formalization of colonial penetration into Africa.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: The 1989 Nigerian Constitution provided for a
explanation: The 1989 Nigerian Constitution provided for a two-party system. A two-party system is a form of party system where two major political parties dominate the political landscape. In the context of Nigeria, the two parties were the Social Democratic Party (SDP) and the National Republican Convention (NRC).

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: The Nigerian federation is a
explanation: The Nigerian federation is a three-tier system. A three-tier system of government is a type of federation in which the federal government, state governments, and local government councils share powers. In Nigeria, these are the Federal, State, and Local Government levels.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM : TIERS OF GOVERNMENT AND THEIR RELATIONSHIP

subject: Government
q: The arm of government mainly responsible for the implementation of law in Nigeria is the
explanation: The executive arm of government is mainly responsible for the implementation of laws in Nigeria. The executive includes the president, vice president, and the cabinet. They are responsible for enforcing laws and maintaining order. The judiciary interprets the laws, the legislature makes the laws, and the police enforce the laws, but the executive is the one that implements them.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The British colonial rule in Nigeria brought about
options: a change in the mode of production
stabilization in the price of raw materials
explanation: The British colonial rule in Nigeria brought about a change in the mode of production. Before the British rule, Nigerians were mainly engaged in subsistence farming and local crafts. However, the British introduced cash crop farming and mining, which changed the mode of production from subsistence to commercial. This led to a significant change in the economic structure of the society.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : IMPACT OF BRITISH COLONIAL RULE- ECONOMIC, POLITICAL, SOCIO-CULTURAL

subject: Government
q: A primary objective of the privatization policy in Nigeria is
explanation: The primary objective of the privatization policy in Nigeria is to reduce the involvement of the state in the economy, increase efficiency and productivity, and attract foreign investment.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following is saddled with the conduct of general elections in Nigeria's Fourth Republic?
explanation: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) is the body saddled with the conduct of general elections in Nigeria's Fourth Republic. It was established in 1998 and is responsible for organizing elections into various political offices in the country.

topics.title: THE ELECTORAL PROCESS

subject: Government
q: Public corporations are established to
explanation: Public corporations are established by the government to provide essential services to the public. They are not primarily focused on reducing government spending or boosting government revenue, but rather on improving the provision of social services. This can include things like transportation, utilities, and healthcare. Therefore, the correct answer is 'improve provision of social appointments'.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: The political structure of Nigeria under the first military regime was
explanation: The political structure of Nigeria under the first military regime was unitary. In a unitary system, all powers are concentrated in the central government, and the states or provinces are subordinate. This was the case in Nigeria under the first military regime, where the Federal Military Government held all the powers. Therefore, the correct answer is 'unitary'.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM

subject: Government
q: The Inspector General of Police is appointed by the executive on the advice of the
explanation: The Inspector General of Police in Nigeria is appointed by the President on the advice of the Nigerian Police Council. The Nigerian Police Council is a constitutional body that advises the President on the administration of the Nigeria Police Force. It is composed of the President as the chairman, the Inspector General of Police, the Chairman of the Police Service Commission, and the Governors of each of the states of the Federation.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is
explanation: A major problem associated with the Nigerian federalism is fiscal allocation. Fiscal allocation refers to the distribution of resources among the different levels of government. In Nigeria, there have been persistent issues with the allocation of resources, with many states and local governments claiming that the federal government retains a disproportionate share of the country's wealth. This has led to conflicts and calls for a review of the revenue allocation formula.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM

subject: Government
q: The six African members of the Nigerian Council were nominated by the
explanation: The six African members of the Nigerian Council were nominated by the Governor. The Nigerian Council was a legislative body established by the British colonial authorities in Nigeria. The Governor, who was the representative of the British Crown, had the power to nominate members to the council.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : OLIVER LYTTLETON CONSTITUTION 1954

subject: Government
q: In the Second Republic, the total membership of each House of Assembly was determined by the
explanation: In the Second Republic, the total membership of each House of Assembly was determined by the Constitution. The Constitution of a country is the supreme law of the land and it stipulates the structure and operation of the government, including the composition of the legislative houses.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : SECOND REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: Privatization and commercialization programmes in Nigeria have
explanation: Privatization and commercialization programmes in Nigeria have de-emphasised government ownership. Privatization is the process of transferring an enterprise or industry from the public sector to the private sector. The government ceases to be the owner of the entity or business. Commercialization is the management or running of an entity in such a way as to make it profitable. It involves the restructuring of a public enterprise to operate as a profit-making commercial entity. Both processes reduce the role of government in controlling and managing the economy.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: The fear of domination in the public service led to the
options: adoption of population as a formula for revenue allocation
explanation: The fear of domination in the public service led to the establishment of the Federal Character Commission. The Federal Character Commission is a Nigerian quasi-federal government body, established to promote, monitor and enforce compliance with the principles of the proportional sharing of all bureaucratic, economic, media and political posts at all levels of government. It was created to prevent the domination of ethnic groups in public service and to ensure equal representation of all states or ethnic groups.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the
explanation: In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the 'ofo'. The 'ofo' is a symbol of authority and leadership in the Igbo traditional political system. It is held by the most senior member of the council of elders, who is seen as the custodian of the community's customs and traditions.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS : IGBO PRE-COLONIAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was
explanation: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup d'etat in Nigeria was 'political instability'. The period leading up to the coup was marked by a high level of political instability characterized by violence, rigging of elections, and ethnic tensions. While issues like corruption, nepotism, and military ambition were also present, they were underlying causes. The immediate trigger was the unstable political environment.

topics.title: THE MILITARY IN NIGERIAN POLITICS : FACTORS THAT LED TO MILITARY INTERVENTION

subject: Government
q: The exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the
explanation: The exclusive list in a federal system of government is a list of items on which only the federal government can make laws. The items on this list are usually issues of national importance or those that require a uniform system across the country. Therefore, the exclusive list is reserved for legislation by the federal government.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The participation of Nigeria in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the
explanation: The participation of Nigeria in the Second World War helped to create an awareness among the nationalists that the Europeans were not superior. This is because they saw that the Europeans also suffered and struggled in the war, just like any other human beings. This realization helped to fuel the desire for independence among the nationalists.

topics.title: PROCESS OF DECOLONIZATION : NATIONALISM – MEANING, TYPES

subject: Government
q: Which of the following mostly militates against the efficiency and productivity of public corporations in Nigeria?
options: The management teams are always appointed at will
members of the management team are always at loggerheads
explanation: The efficiency and productivity of public corporations in Nigeria are mostly militated against by incessant interference by the government. This is because the government, as the major stakeholder, often interferes in the operations of these corporations, thereby affecting their efficiency and productivity. The other options may also affect the efficiency and productivity of public corporations but not as much as government interference.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS : PROBLEMS

subject: Government
q: The power to nominate members of the judiciary at the federal level under the 1999 Constitution is vested in the
explanation: The power to nominate members of the judiciary at the federal level under the 1999 Constitution is vested in the National Judicial Council. The National Judicial Council is a body established by the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria to protect the Judiciary's independence. It is responsible for the appointment, promotion, and discipline of judicial officers. The Federal Judicial Service Commission is responsible for advising the National Judicial Council on suitable candidates for nomination to the judiciary. The Supreme Court and the Nigerian Bar Association do not have the power to nominate members of the judiciary.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: Prior to 1976, the local government system practised in Northern Nigeria was
explanation: Prior to 1976, the local government system practiced in Northern Nigeria was the 'Chief-in-Council' system. This system was characterized by a traditional ruler (the Chief) who made decisions in consultation with a council of elders or notable individuals within the community.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: In the segemented Tiv society, governance was usually confined to the
explanation: In the segmented Tiv society, governance was usually confined to the 'clan'. The Tiv society is traditionally a highly decentralized society with no centralized authority. Instead, authority and governance are distributed among various clans, each of which is autonomous and self-governing.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following influenced nationalist activities in Nigeria?
explanation: The Pan-African Congress influenced nationalist activities in Nigeria. The Pan-African Congress was a series of meetings that focused on addressing the issues facing Africa as a result of European colonization. The congresses, held from 1900 to 1945, were significant in the sense that they connected people of African descent from around the globe, and they were a key factor in the push for decolonization and independence of African countries, including Nigeria.

topics.title: PROCESS OF DECOLONIZATION : NATIONALIST MOVEMENTS – EMERGENCE, GOALS, STRATEGIES

subject: Government
q: The party that did not control any state in the Second Republic was
explanation: The People's Redemption Party (PRP) did not control any state in the Second Republic. The Second Republic in Nigeria was a period of democratic rule from 1979 to 1983. During this time, several political parties were formed, and they competed for control of the states. However, the PRP was not able to secure control of any state.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : SECOND REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: 'Pacification' refers to a process through which some areas in Nigeria were acquired by
explanation: 'Pacification' refers to the process of subduing a particular area or region through force, often in the context of colonialism. In the case of Nigeria, this was done through military conquest. The British used military force to subdue and control various regions of Nigeria, particularly in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. This process is often referred to as 'pacification'.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: Counter-trade was introduced by the government led by
explanation: Counter-trade is a form of international trade in which goods or services are exchanged for other goods or services rather than for hard currency. This type of trade is often used when hard currency is difficult to obtain or when countries want to bypass foreign exchange controls. In Nigeria, counter-trade was introduced during the regime of General Ibrahim Babangida in the 1980s as a response to the economic crisis and foreign debt.

topics.title: ECONOMIC POLICIES AND DEVELOPMENT STRATEGIES : COUNTER-TRADE

subject: Government
q: A major limitation of the Commonwealth is that it lacks
options: the power to set formula rules to govern the affairs of member-nations
an organizational structure and a definite identity
the power to compel member-nations to comply with its decisions
explanation: The Commonwealth operates on a consensus model, meaning that decisions are made collectively and member nations are not compelled to follow them. Therefore, it lacks the power to compel member-nations to comply with its decisions.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : COMMONWEALTH

subject: Government
q: Which of the following countries has withdrawn her membership from ECOWAS
explanation: Mauritania withdrew from ECOWAS in 2000. The question is self explanatory in relation to the answer.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : ECOWAS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following is a major threat to OPEC?
options: Persistence of war in the Middle East
The discovery of crude oil in more countries
Development of valuable alternative to crude oil
explanation: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is a group of oil-producing nations that aim to ensure a steady income to the member states and to secure reliable supply of oil to the consumers. The development of valuable alternatives to crude oil is a major threat to OPEC because it could reduce the global demand for oil, thereby affecting the income of the member states. This is because alternatives to crude oil such as renewable energy sources (solar, wind, etc.) are becoming more popular due to environmental concerns and technological advancements.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : OPEC

subject: Government
q: The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as
explanation: The African Union (AU) is a supranational organization. A supranational organization is a type of multinational political union where negotiated power is delegated to an authority by governments of member states. The term is sometimes used to describe the European Union (EU), as a new type of political entity. The AU's right to intervene in a member country in crisis portrays it as a supranational organization because it has the power to make decisions and take actions that could override the sovereignty of its member countries in certain situations.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : AU

subject: Government
q: The permanent membership of the UN Security Council is
explanation: The United Nations Security Council is composed of 15 members, but only five of them are permanent. These five are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States. These countries were the main allies in World War II and have maintained the world's most powerful economies since then.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : UNO

subject: Government
q: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for
explanation: A flexible constitution is one that can be easily amended or changed. This is an advantage because it allows for the constitution to adapt to changing circumstances and needs. It can be updated to reflect societal changes, technological advancements, or shifts in political climate. This flexibility can help to prevent constitutional crises and maintain the relevance and effectiveness of the constitution.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: Power refers to the
options: ability to impose one's will upon others
duties political parties demand of their registered members
explanation: Power, in the context of politics and governance, refers to the ability to influence others or impose one's will upon others. It is the capacity or ability to direct or influence the behavior of others or the course of events. None of the other options accurately define the concept of power.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: Prorogation is the bringing of a session of a parliament to an end through
explanation: Prorogation is the formal ending of a parliamentary session and it is typically done through a royal proclamation. It is the discontinuation of a session of a parliament or other legislative assembly without dissolving it. It is not brought about by final reading, guillotine, or impeachment.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The right of all eligible citizens to vote and be voted for is referred to as
explanation: The right of all eligible citizens to vote and be voted for is referred to as universal suffrage. Universal suffrage means that the right to vote should not be restricted by race, sex, belief, wealth, or social status. The term is associated with the expansion of voting rights to adult citizens.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : CITIZENSHIP RIGHTS

subject: Government
q: Values, beliefs, and orientation of a political system are internalized through
explanation: Values, beliefs, and orientation of a political system are internalized through political socialization. Political socialization is a lifelong process by which people form their ideas about politics and acquire political values. The family, educational system, and mass media are important socializing agents.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

subject: Government
q: Occupational pressure groups pursue their objectives mainly by
explanation: Occupational pressure groups, also known as interest groups, are organizations that seek to influence government policy, public opinion, or business decisions. They pursue their objectives mainly by issuing ultimatums and embarking on strikes. This is a common method used to exert pressure on the government or businesses to meet their demands. They do not typically alienate the government, embark on guerrilla warfare, or contest elections as these are not effective or appropriate methods for achieving their goals.

topics.title: PRESSURE GROUPS

subject: Government
q: In a cabinet system, the legislature comprises the
options: prime minister and members of the parliament
prime minister and member of the jury
explanation: In a cabinet system of government, the legislature comprises the prime minister and members of the parliament. The prime minister is the head of government and the members of parliament are the elected representatives of the people. They work together to make laws and govern the country. The cabinet system does not include members of the jury, ministers, or the president in the legislature.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The committee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature is the
explanation: The Appropriation Committee is the committee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature. This committee is responsible for examining the estimates of government expenditure submitted by the Finance Minister in his budget and suggesting economies in public expenditure. The other committees listed do not have this specific function.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: Democracy is a system of government that is
options: constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens
imposed on the majority of the citizens
put in place to favour the party caucus
explanation: Democracy is a system of government that is constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens. In a democracy, the power to govern is derived from the people, who express their will through elections. The other options do not accurately describe the nature of a democratic system.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : DEMOCRACY FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: Collective ownership of property is associated with
explanation: Collective ownership of property is a key principle of communism. In a communist society, all property is publicly owned and each person works and is paid according to their abilities and needs. This is different from federalism, mercantilism, and capitalism where property is privately owned.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : COMMUNISM

subject: Government
q: The agency responsible for investigating allegations of maladministration in the civil service is the
explanation: The Public Complaints Commission, also known as the Ombudsman, is the agency responsible for investigating allegations of maladministration in the civil service. It serves as a watchdog to ensure administrative justice in public offices. The Federal Civil Services Commission is responsible for appointment, promotion, and discipline in the civil service. The Code of Conduct Bureau is responsible for ensuring that public officers adhere to the code of conduct for public officers. The Federal Character Commission ensures fair and equitable distribution of posts and socio-economic amenities among the federating units of Nigeria.

topics.title: PUBLIC COMMISSIONS ESTABLISHED BY THE 1979 AND SUBSEQUENT CONSTITUTIONS : THE PUBLIC COMPLAINTS COMMISSION

subject: Government
q: A political entity that possesses independence is a
explanation: A political entity that possesses independence is a state. A state is a political entity that possesses a permanent population, a defined territory, a government, and the capacity to enter into relations with other states. It is independent because it has sovereignty, which means it has supreme power or authority over its territory and is not controlled by an outside force.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : SOCIETY, STATE, NATION AND NATION-STATE

subject: Government
q: The principles that advocates strict adherence to the fundamental laws of a country is
explanation: The principle that advocates strict adherence to the fundamental laws of a country is constitutionalism. Constitutionalism is a political philosophy that asserts that governance should be conducted according to an established set of laws, such as a constitution. It is the idea that government should be limited in its powers and that its authority depends on its observation of these laws.

topics.title: PRINCIPLES OF DEMOCRATIC GOVERNMENT : CONSTITUTIONALISM, RULE OF LAW AND REPRESENTATIVE GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: One advantage of the unitary systems over federal system is its
explanation: A unitary system of government is a sovereign state governed as a single entity. The central government is supreme, and the administrative divisions exercise only powers that the central government has delegated to them. This makes it more cost-effective as it avoids duplication of government functions in multiple levels of government, unlike in a federal system where there is a division of powers between the central government and the constituent units.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE : UNITARY FEATURES, REASONS FOR ADOPTION, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following is a duty of an electoral commission?
explanation: An electoral commission is a body responsible for administering elections. One of its key duties is the registration of political parties. It does not create political parties, establish electoral tribunals, or inaugurate the parliament. These are functions of other arms of government.

topics.title: THE ELECTORAL PROCESS : ELECTORAL COMMISSION – FUNCTIONS, PROBLEMS.

subject: Government
q: A form of government in which the nobles rule for the benefit of all is
explanation: Aristocracy is a form of government in which power is in the hands of a small, privileged, ruling class, the nobles. The nobles rule with the understanding that they are to use their power and influence for the benefit of all citizens, not just their own class.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : ARISTOCRACY FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: Fascism as an ideology facilitates
explanation: Fascism is a political ideology that is marked by dictatorial power, forcible suppression of opposition, and strong regimentation of society and of the economy which is also known as totalitarianism. Therefore, fascism facilitates totalitarianism.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : FASCISM

subject: Government
q: A prominent feature of a presidential system of government is
explanation: A prominent feature of a presidential system of government is the 'separation of powers'. This means that the executive, legislative, and judicial branches of government are distinct, with each having its own responsibilities. This system prevents any one branch from gaining too much power and ensures a balance of power.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The executive checks the excesses of the legislature through
explanation: The executive checks the excesses of the legislature through 'the use of veto powers'. The veto power allows the executive (the president or governor, for example) to refuse to sign a bill into law. This is a key check and balance in a democratic system, as it prevents the legislature from passing laws without the agreement of the executive.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The consciousness of a common identity of a people is called
explanation: The consciousness of a common identity of a people is called nationalism. Nationalism is a political ideology that involves a strong identification of a group of individuals with a political entity defined in national terms, i.e. a nation. It is the sense of a common identity, including language, culture, and shared experiences, among a nation's people.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : SOCIETY, STATE, NATION AND NATION-STATE

subject: Government
q: Citizenship of a state by descent requires that
options: one is born and bred in the state
one's mother was born in the state
one's grandparents were citizens of the state
explanation: Citizenship of a state by descent requires that one's grandparents were citizens of the state. This is known as jus sanguinis, or right of blood, which is a principle of nationality law by which citizenship is determined or acquired by the nationality or ethnicity of one or both parents. This means that you are considered a citizen of a country if your parents or grandparents were citizens, regardless of where you were born.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Government
q: In order to dispense justice impartially, the judiciary primarily needs
explanation: The judiciary needs independence primarily to dispense justice impartially. Independence ensures that the judiciary is not influenced by external forces such as the executive or legislative branches of government, or by personal interests. This allows the judiciary to make decisions based solely on the law and the facts of each case, ensuring fair and impartial justice.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: A state with a hegemonic political party is one in which
explanation: A state with a hegemonic political party is one in which there is one dominant party. This does not necessarily mean that there are no other parties, but rather that one party has overwhelming influence or control over the political landscape. This dominance could be due to a variety of factors, such as popular support, control of resources, or institutional advantages.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY SYSTEMS : PARTY SYSTEMS – DEFINITION, ORGANIZATION, FUNCTIONS.

subject: Government
q: Nigeria's committment to the liberation of Africa could best be seen in her policy towards
explanation: Nigeria's commitment to the liberation of Africa was most evident in her policy towards South Africa and Angola. During the apartheid era, Nigeria was one of the foremost supporters of anti-apartheid movements, including the African National Congress. The country also provided substantial support to the Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola (MPLA), which was fighting for independence from Portugal. This support was in line with Nigeria's foreign policy, which was heavily focused on decolonization and anti-apartheid efforts in Africa.

topics.title: NIGERIA IN INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : THE AFRICAN UNION

subject: Government
q: The set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another is called
explanation: Foreign policy refers to the set of policies on the basis of which countries interact with one another. It involves making choices about relations with other countries. Foreign policy includes diplomacy, security, international trade policies, and humanitarian efforts. It is designed by the government to achieve complex domestic and international goals. It protects the national interest, national security, ideological goals, and economic prosperity. The other options do not accurately describe the set of policies that guide international relations.

topics.title: FOREIGN POLICY : DEFINITION AND PURPOSE

subject: Government
q: Nigerian membership of the UN complements her foreign policy in the area of
explanation: Nigeria's membership of the United Nations (UN) complements her foreign policy in the area of multilateral diplomacy. Multilateral diplomacy refers to the practice of involving more than two nations or parties in achieving diplomatic solutions to international challenges. The UN is a global organization that facilitates multilateral diplomacy by providing a platform for dialogue and negotiation among its member states.

topics.title: NIGERIA IN INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : THE UNITED NATIONS

subject: Government
q: Nigerian teamed up with other developing countries to assert a neutral posture under the platform of
explanation: Nigeria teamed up with other developing countries to assert a neutral posture under the platform of the Non-aligned Movement. The Non-aligned Movement was a group of states which were not formally aligned with or against any major power bloc during the Cold War. It provided a platform for countries to ensure their independent policy stance, free from the influence of major powers.

topics.title: NIGERIA’S FOREIGN POLICY : NIGERIA’S NON-ALIGNMENT POLICY

subject: Government
q: Nation-State is synonymous with
explanation: Sovereignty is the highest power of a country to make and enforce laws within its borders without foreign influence. This is synonymous with a Nation-State.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : SOCIETY, STATE, NATION AND NATION-STATE

subject: Government
q: A fundamental component of political culture is
explanation: A fundamental component of political culture is social values. The components of political culture are: Cognitive orientation, Evaluative and Affective orientation.
