q: One of the measures taken in 1988 to grant more autonomy to the local governments in Nigeria was the
options: abolition of the state ministries of local government
creation of the post of supervisory councillors
creation of the post of chairman of local government
abolition of its supervision by state government
explanation: In 1988, one of the measures taken to grant more autonomy to the local governments in Nigeria was the abolition of the state ministries of local government. This was done to reduce the control of the state government over the local government and allow them to operate more independently.

topics.title: LOCAL GOVERNMENT :FEATURES OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT REFORMS 1976, 1989 - STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS, FINANCE AND INTER-GOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS

subject: Government
q: The 1963 Constitution of Nigeria was
explanation: The 1963 Constitution of Nigeria was written and rigid. A rigid constitution is one that requires a special procedure for its amendment, unlike a flexible constitution which can be easily amended. The 1963 Constitution of Nigeria required such special procedure for its amendment, hence it is considered rigid.

topics.title: POST - INDEPENDENCE CONSTITUTIONS : 1963 CONSTITUTION CHARACTERISTICS AND SHORTCOMINGS

subject: Government
q: The doctrine of rule of law is associated with
explanation:
In the 19th century, A. V. Dicey, a constitutional scholar and lawyer, wrote of the twin pillars of the British constitution in his classic work Introduction to the Study of the Law of the Constitution (1885); these two pillars are the rule of law and parliamentary sovereignty.

Dicey Rule of Law: The concept of rule of law backs to the time of Aristotle. Aristotle ruled out the concept of rule under discretion by all means and tried to convey his followers that given the choice it is always rule of law that scores over rule of discretion.

In Modern times the rule of law was propounded by the Albert Dicey, a British jurist and Philosopher. He gave following three postulates of rule of law:
* Everyone is equal before the law.
* Sanctions have to be backed by law.
* Courts are the ultimate body and supremacy of court is ambivalent in civilized society.

He was firm proponent of the concept and very influential thinker of his times.

topics.title: PRINCIPLES OF DEMOCRATIC GOVERNMENT : CONSTITUTIONALISM, RULE OF LAW AND REPRESENTATIVE GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: Nigerian foreign policy implementation is the responsibility of the
explanation: The implementation of Nigerian foreign policy is the responsibility of the Ministry of External Affairs. The Ministry of External Affairs is the government body responsible for the conduct of foreign relations of Nigeria. This includes implementing foreign policy decisions, coordinating and supervising all activities of the government relating to foreign relations, and promoting Nigerian interests abroad.

topics.title: FOREIGN POLICY : FORMULATION AND IMPLEMENTATION

subject: Government
q: Nigeria is NOT a member of the
explanation: The mission of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is to promote policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people around the world. It was established in 1961. Nigeria is not a member.

topics.title: NIGERIA IN INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : ORGANISATION OF PETROLEUM EXPORTING COUNTRIES

subject: Government
q: The major function of the legislative assembly is to
explanation: The major function of the legislative assembly is to make laws. While the legislative assembly does debate on committee reports, represent the people, and vote on bills, its primary function is to create laws that govern the nation. This is done through a process of drafting, debating, and voting on bills, which, if passed, become law.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The process of law-making by the legislature begins with
explanation: The process of law-making by the legislature, also known as the legislative process, begins with the initiation of a bill. A bill is a proposal for a new law, or a proposal to change an existing law, presented for debate and approval. Once a bill has been drafted, it goes through several stages of review and discussion before it becomes law. The first stage in this process is the initiation of the bill, where the idea for the law is proposed and the bill is drafted.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION

subject: Government
q: A device for controlling prolonged debates in parliament is called
explanation: Cloture closure, or, informally, a guillotine is a motion or process in parliamentary procedure aimed at bringing debate to a quick end.

topics.title: LEGISLATURE

subject: Government
q: A problem of public corporations in Nigeria is
explanation: Public corporations in Nigeria often suffer from wastage of resources. This is due to factors such as corruption, inefficiency, and lack of proper management. Public control, emphasis, and choice of leadership are not inherently problems of public corporations.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: Privatization and commercialization of public enterprises in Nigeria will lead to further entrenchment of
explanation: Privatization and commercialization of public enterprises in Nigeria will lead to further entrenchment of capitalism. This is because privatization involves the transfer of ownership, management, and control of public enterprises to the private sector, which operates on the principles of capitalism. Socialism, welfarism, and fascism are not the economic systems that are promoted by privatization and commercialization.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: The Native Authority system was most effective and successful in
explanation: The Native Authority system was most effective and successful in Northern Nigeria. This is because the system was built on the already existing traditional administrative system in the North, which was highly centralized under the Emirs. The British colonial masters found it easy to use this system to their advantage.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

q: In orientation, early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially
explanation: It was first reformist. The aim was to build consensus for a later soft revolution which led to Independence.

subject: Government
q: Laws made by military government at the state level are called
explanation: Laws made by military government at the state level are called edicts. An edict is a decree or proclamation issued by an authority and having the force of law. In a military government, the military authority often issues these edicts.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION : DECREES

subject: Government
q: Laws made by military state governments are known as
explanation: Laws made by military state governments are known as edicts. As explained earlier, an edict is a decree or proclamation issued by an authority and having the force of law. In a military government, the military authority often issues these edicts.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION : DECREES

q: ECOWAS is aimed at uniting the West African States
explanation: ECOWAS was established to promote cooperation and integration in order to create an economic and monetary union for promoting economic growth and development in West Africa.

q: According to Marxist theory, those who own and control the means of production in a capitalist society are
explanation: According to Marxist theories, bourgeoisie the capitalist class who own most of society's wealth and means of production.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : CAPITALISM

subject: Government
q: The development of a classless society is the goal of
explanation: The development of a classless society is the goal of Marxism. Marxism is a social, political, and economic theory originated by Karl Marx, which focuses on the struggle between capitalists and the working class. Marx believed that societal change happens as a result of this class struggle, and his ultimate goal was a society in which there are no classes, no private property, and no state.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : SOCIALISM

subject: Government
q: The emergence of nationalism was essentially the result of the bills of
explanation: The emergence of nationalism was essentially the result of colonialism. Colonialism is the policy or practice of acquiring full or partial political control over another country, occupying it with settlers, and exploiting it economically. The experience of colonial rule provoked nationalist movements in various parts of the world as people sought to regain their independence and sovereignty.

topics.title: PROCESS OF DECOLONIZATION : NATIONALIST MOVEMENTS – EMERGENCE, GOALS, STRATEGIES

q: In 1958, ...... moved a motion for self - government in Nigeria
explanation: In August 1958, Remi Fani-Kayode revisited Enahoro's motion and the motion was again passed by parliament but its date was not approved by the British.

subject: Government
q: A major influence on the formulation of public opinion is
explanation: The mass media is a major influence on the formulation of public opinion. This is because the mass media has the ability to reach a large number of people and thus has a significant impact on shaping public opinion. It includes various forms of communication such as television, radio, newspapers, magazines, and the internet. The mass media provides information, interpretation, and commentary on a variety of issues, thereby influencing people's perceptions and opinions.

topics.title: PUBLIC OPINION

subject: Government
q: An important principle of the Civil service is
explanation: The anonymity of the civil service is linked to two concepts: permanence and neutrality. Civil servants, many of whom remain in their jobs whilst serving numerous governments, are thus likely to have to give advice to governments of different political parties, who may have different attitudes to policy.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: Parastatals are established to
explanation: Parastatals, also known as public corporations or state-owned enterprises, are established to 'render social services'. They are created by the government to provide services that are either not fully addressed by the private sector or where the government feels the service is so important that control cannot be risked by leaving it to the private sector. These services can include transportation, utilities, and healthcare.

topics.title: PUBLIC CORPORATIONS AND PARASTATALS

subject: Government
q: One of the obligations of a citizen is
options: obedience to the laws of the land
explanation: One of the obligations of a citizen is to obey the laws of the land. This is because laws are made to maintain order and protect the rights and freedoms of all citizens. If citizens do not obey the laws, there would be chaos and disorder. Therefore, obedience to the laws of the land is a fundamental obligation of every citizen.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : DUTIES AND OBLIGATIONS OF CITIZENS

subject: Government
q: One of the obligations of a citizen is
options: obedience to the laws of the land
explanation: One of the obligations of a citizen is to obey the laws of the land. This is because laws are made to maintain order and protect the rights and freedoms of all citizens. If citizens do not obey the laws, there would be chaos and disorder. Therefore, obedience to the laws of the land is a fundamental obligation of every citizen.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : DUTIES AND OBLIGATIONS OF CITIZENS

subject: Government
q: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is
explanation: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is suffrage. Suffrage is the right to vote in political elections. It is a fundamental principle of democracy that gives citizens the power to choose their representatives in the government.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : CITIZENSHIP RIGHTS

subject: Government
q: The major advantage of the secret ballot is that
options: it ensures the anonymity of each voter
losers can ask for another secret vote
explanation: The major advantage of the secret ballot is that it ensures the anonymity of each voter. A secret ballot is a voting method in which a voter's choices in an election or a referendum are anonymous. This forestalls attempts to influence the voter by intimidation, blackmailing, and potential vote buying.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : CITIZENSHIP RIGHTS

subject: Government
q: Groups which seek to bring about changes in government policies for employees are known as ......
explanation: Pressure groups are organizations that attempt to influence government policies. They do not seek to govern a country but rather to influence the decisions of those who do. They can be focused on a range of issues, including employee rights, and often lobby politicians and organize campaigns to achieve their goals. Therefore, groups which seek to bring about changes in government policies for employees are known as pressure groups.

topics.title: PRESSURE GROUPS

subject: Government
q: Which of the following countries does NOT operate a federal constitution?
explanation: A federal constitution is a system of government in which power is divided between a central authority and constituent political units, like states or provinces. The U.S.A, Canada, and Nigeria all operate under a federal constitution. France, however, operates under a unitary system of government where the central government holds the majority of the power, and any powers held by subnational entities are delegated by the central government. Therefore, France does not operate a federal constitution.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: In the botched Third Republic, the unit of representation in the House of Representatives was the
explanation: In the botched Third Republic of Nigeria, the unit of representation in the House of Representatives was the electoral ward. The electoral ward is a subdivision of a local government area, used for electoral purposes. Each ward is represented by a councillor who is elected by the residents of the ward.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : THIRD REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The reason behind Nigeria's suspension from the Commonwealth in 1995 was
explanation: Nigeria was suspended from the Commonwealth in 1995 due to political reasons. Specifically, the suspension was a response to the execution of Ken Saro-Wiwa and eight other Ogoni activists by the military government of General Sani Abacha, which was widely condemned internationally. The Commonwealth is a political association of 54 member states, nearly all of which are former territories of the British Empire. The chief institutions of the organisation are the Commonwealth Secretariat, which focuses on intergovernmental aspects, and the Commonwealth Foundation, which focuses on non-governmental relations amongst member states.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : COMMONWEALTH

subject: Government
q: The agent through which the state undertakes political socialization is the
explanation: Political socialization is the process by which individuals learn and frequently internalize a political lens framing their perceptions of how power is arranged and how the world around them is (and should be) organized. Schools play a significant role in this process by teaching students about the structure and function of government, the rights and responsibilities of citizens, and the values and beliefs that underpin democratic societies.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

subject: Government
q: In which of the following states was the governor impeached during the second republic?
explanation: During Nigeria's Second Republic (1979-1983), the governor of Anambra State, Jim Nwobodo, was impeached.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : SECOND REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The NCNC and the NPC facilitated the creation of the
explanation: The National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons (NCNC) and the Northern People's Congress (NPC) facilitated the creation of the Mid-West Region in Nigeria. The Mid-West Region was created in 1963 from the Western Region. It was the first time a new region was created in Nigeria that was not based on ethnic lines.

topics.title: INSTITUTIONS OF GOVERNMENT IN THE POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : THE LEGISLATIVE – STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND WORKING.

subject: Government
q: The Nigerian federalism is a
explanation: The Nigerian federalism is a three-tier system. This system is made up of the Federal Government, the State Governments, and the Local Government Areas. The Federal Government is responsible for issues that affect the entire country, such as defense and foreign policy. The State Governments handle issues that are specific to their states, such as education and health. The Local Government Areas handle local issues, such as waste management and local infrastructure.

topics.title: THE STRUCTURE AND WORKINGS OF NIGERIAN FEDERALISM : RATIONALE FOR A FEDERAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: The first general election in Nigeria was held in
explanation: Parliamentary elections were held in Nigeria on 12 December 1959. The result was a victory for the Northern People's Congress, which won 134 of the 312 seats in the House of Representatives, despite the Action Group winning more votes.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FIRST REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The option A - 4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the
explanation: It was adopted during the botched Third Republic. The question is self explanatory in relation to the answer.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FOURTH REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The separation of judicial, legislative and executive functions is designed to
explanation: The separation of powers into judicial, legislative, and executive branches is a fundamental concept in the constitution of a democratic state. This separation is designed to prevent tyranny by ensuring that no single entity or individual has absolute power. Each branch has its own responsibilities and can check and balance the others, which prevents any one branch from becoming too powerful.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : SEPARATION OF POWERS

subject: Government
q: In a democracy, ultimate authority resides in the head of state who is also the
explanation: In a democratic system, the ultimate authority does not reside in the head of state but in the people. However, the head of state often holds the position of the commander-in-chief of the armed forces. This does not mean they have ultimate authority, but they do have significant power. The chief justice, senate president, and speaker of the house are all important roles, but they do not hold ultimate authority in a democracy.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : SEPARATION OF POWERS

subject: Government
q: The Civil service cadre responsible for the general supervision and control of ministries is the
explanation: The administrative cadre in the civil service is responsible for the general supervision and control of ministries. They are the ones who make the major decisions and oversee the operations of the ministries. The professional cadre are specialists in their respective fields, the executive cadre are responsible for implementing decisions and the clerical cadre are responsible for routine office tasks.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: The Civil service embraces all workers in
explanation: The Civil service embraces all workers in government ministries. Public corporations are not part of the civil service, they are separate entities that are owned by the government. Private corporations and companies are not part of the civil service as they are not owned or controlled by the government.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: The rule of law is a cardinal principle associated with
explanation: The rule of law is a fundamental principle in democratic systems. It means that all individuals, institutions, and entities, public and private, including the State itself, are accountable to laws that are publicly promulgated, equally enforced, and independently adjudicated. It is the principle that the law should govern a nation, as opposed to being governed by arbitrary decisions of individual government officials.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: An unwritten constitution operates in
explanation: An unwritten constitution is a type of constitution where the fundamental rules often take the form of customs, usage, precedent and a variety of statutes and legal instruments. An example of a country with an unwritten constitution is the United Kingdom (Great Britain). It is called unwritten because not all laws are written in a single document.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

subject: Government
q: A characteristic of public opinion is that it is
explanation: Public opinion is dynamic, not static. This means it changes over time as people are exposed to new information and experiences. It is not inherently positive or negative, but can be either depending on the issue at hand.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

subject: Government
q: The agent through which the state undertakes political socialization is the
explanation: Political socialization is the process by which individuals learn and frequently internalize a political lens framing their perceptions of how power is arranged and how the world around them is (and should be) organized. Those political perspectives can shape and influence the individual's perceptions of a wide range of issues. Schools are one of the primary agents through which political socialization occurs. They teach students about the structure of the political system, the values that underpin it, and the rights and responsibilities of citizens.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

q: The option A - 4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the
explanation: It was adopted during the botched Third Republic. The question is self explanatory in relation to the answer.

subject: Government
q: The option A4 model was used in the conduct of the
explanation: The Option A4 model was used in the conduct of the 1993 elections in Nigeria. This system was introduced by the government of General Ibrahim Babangida. The 1993 presidential election, which was presumably won by Moshood Kashimawo Olawale Abiola, was conducted using this system. However, the election was annulled by the same government that conducted it.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FOURTH REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: The formation of confederations is based primarily on
explanation: Confederations are often formed for reasons related to security. This is because a confederation is a union of sovereign states, united for purposes of common action often in relation to other states. They are typically created to deal with critical issues, such as defense, foreign affairs, or a common currency, with the central government being required to provide support for all members.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE : CONFEDERAL FEATURES, REASONS FOR ADOPTION, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: The first Head of Government in independent Nigeria was
explanation: Alhaji Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa, KBE(1) (December 1912 – January 15, 1966) was a Nigerian politician, and the only prime minister of an independent Nigeria.

explanation: In 1953, Enahoro became the first to move the motion for Nigeria's independence which was eventually granted in 1960 after several political setbacks and defeats in parliament. Enahoro has been regarded by academics and many Nigerians as the "Father of Nigeria State".

explanation: Feudalism was a political system in which a lord owned all the land while vassals and serfs farmed it.

subject: Government
q: According to Marxist theory, those who live by selling their labour are referred to as the
explanation: According to Marxist theory, the society is divided into two main classes: the bourgeoisie and the proletariat. The bourgeoisie are the owners of the means of production, such as factories, land, and machinery. They buy the labour of the proletariat, who do not own any means of production and therefore have to sell their labour to survive. Therefore, the correct answer is 'proletariats'.

q: The highest grade in the civil service is known as the
explanation: The highest grade in the civil service is known as the administrative cadre. The civil service is divided into various grades and the administrative cadre is considered the highest. This is because it is the cadre that is responsible for the administration of the entire civil service.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: The political neutrality of civil servants is guaranteed by not allowing them to
explanation: The Civil Service is neutral in its work. It does not interfere in the political arena. They serve whichever political party is in power.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: Commonwealth nations are represented in other member nations by
explanation: Commonwealth nations are represented in other member nations by high commissioners. This is a unique diplomatic practice among Commonwealth countries where they do not exchange ambassadors, but instead have high commissioners who perform the same role as an ambassador. The term 'High Commissioner' is used instead of 'Ambassador' to mark the special, collegial relationship among Commonwealth countries.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : COMMONWEALTH

subject: Government
q: The rights of a citizen can be withdrawn by the state if the person
explanation: The rights of a citizen can be withdrawn by the state if the person is convicted by a court. This is because when a person is convicted of a crime, they may lose certain civil rights such as the right to vote, hold public office, or serve on a jury. This is a legal consequence of conviction known as 'civil death'. The other options do not necessarily result in the loss of citizenship rights.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : CITIZENSHIP RIGHTS

q: One instrument for safeguarding the rights of citizens is
explanation: A writ of habeas corpus (which literally means to "produce the body") is a court order to a person or agency holding someone in custody (such as a warden) to deliver the imprisoned individual to the court issuing the order and to show a valid reason for that person's detention.

q: The 1976 Reforms have been most beneficial to the
explanation: The Local Government Reform 1976 was a nationwide undertaking by the military government as part of its intention to return power to civilians, sort of a precursor to active comprehensive political action, it was also designed to spread government programmes to the grassroots and to institutionalize the local government system in Nigeria. The reform resulted in the creation of 301 local governments across the country of varying populations between 150,000- 800,000.

explanation: The Nigerian Independence Constitution was modified by the 1963 constitution. The 1963 constitution, also known as the Republican Constitution, replaced the 1960 Independence Constitution. It was the constitution of Nigeria from 1963 until the military coup in 1966, which led to the drafting of a new constitution.

explanation: The premier of Western Region immediately after independence was Chief Ladoke Akintola. He took over from Chief Obafemi Awolowo in 1960 when Awolowo resigned to take up a position at the federal level. Akintola served as premier until the military coup in 1966.

explanation: The foreign policy thrust of the Babangida administration was economic diplomacy. Economic diplomacy includes all the economic activities, including but not limited to export, import, investment, lending, aid, free trade agreements, etc.

subject: Government
q: Under military regimes, the branches of government that becomes fused are the
explanation: Under military regimes, the separation of powers, which is a fundamental principle of democracy, is often not observed. The military often controls all branches of government - the executive, the legislature, and the judiciary. This is because the military regime is a form of autocracy where power is concentrated in the hands of a single authority, in this case, the military. Therefore, the correct answer is 'judiciary, the executive and the legislature'.

subject: Government
q: The first military government in Nigeria was headed by
explanation: The first military government in Nigeria was headed by Major General Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi. He took over power after a military coup d'etat that overthrew the first republic on January 15, 1966. The coup led to the death of many political leaders and Ironsi, who was the most senior military officer at the time, took over the reins of government.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : JOHN MACPHERSON CONSTITUTION 1951

subject: Government
q: Educated elites did not like the system of indirect rule in Nigeria because it
explanation: The system of indirect rule was disliked by the educated elites in Nigeria because it was undemocratic and oppressive. The system was introduced by Lord Lugard, the first Governor-General of Nigeria. It involved ruling the local people through their traditional rulers. The traditional rulers were made to be answerable to the British officials. The educated elites saw this system as undemocratic because it did not involve the participation of the people in their governance. It was also seen as oppressive because it gave the traditional rulers absolute power and they often abused this power.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH COLONIAL ADMINISTRATIVE POLICY – DIRECT AND INDIRECT RULE

subject: Government
q: Who was the architect of the British rule in Nigeria?
explanation: Sir Frederick Lugard between was a British soldier, mercenary, explorer of Africa and colonial administrator. He was Governor of Hong Kong (1907–1912), the last Governor of the Southern Nigeria Protectorate (1912–1914), the first High Commissioner (1900–1906) and last Governor (1912–1914) of the Northern Nigeria Protectorate and the first Governor-General of Nigeria (1914–1919).

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: The standing committee of a legislature is one
explanation: A standing committee in a legislature is a committee that has statutory responsibilities. It is a permanent committee that exists over time, unlike ad-hoc committees which are temporary. Standing committees are responsible for specific areas of public policy, legislation, and oversight of the executive. They consider bills and issues and oversee agencies, programs, and activities within their jurisdictions.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The standing committee of a legislature is one
explanation: A standing committee in a legislature is a committee that has statutory responsibilities. It is a permanent committee that meets regularly and is responsible for a particular area of policy. The term 'standing' refers to the fact that these committees are permanent, not that their members stand while deliberating. They do not perform ad-hoc functions, and they do not necessarily include all legislators as members.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: In liberal democracies, elections help to
options: identify the richest groups in the country
explanation: In liberal democracies, elections serve to determine the acceptability of the government. They are a means for citizens to choose their representatives in the government and express their approval or disapproval of their government's policies. They do not necessarily unite the country against outside groups, create goodwill throughout the nation, or identify the richest groups in the country.

explanation:

explanation: Pressure groups interest is to influence public policies. Political parties aim to influence public opinion to get voted into power.

subject: Government
q: The expenditure of public funds by the executive in Nigeria is controlled by the
explanation: The expenditure of public funds by the executive in Nigeria is controlled by the legislature. This is because the legislature is responsible for approving the budget, which includes all public spending. The executive can propose a budget, but it is the legislature that has the final say on whether it is approved. This is a key aspect of the separation of powers, which is a fundamental principle of democratic governance.

explanation: It was in 1976 under the regime of General Murtala Muhammed who created additional 7 states, from the 12 existing states making it 19.

q: The NCNC and the NPC facilitated the creation of the
explanation: The National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons (NCNC) and the Northern People's Congress (NPC) facilitated the creation of the Mid-West Region in Nigeria. The Mid-West Region was created in 1963 from the Western Region. It was the first time a new region was created in Nigeria that was not based on ethnic lines.

explanation: Professor Humphrey Nwosu (born 2 October 1941) was chairman of the National Electoral Commission of Nigeria (NECON) appointed by President Ibrahim Babangida, holding office from 1989 to 1993.

explanation: Nigeria's foreign policy is governed by principles such as National interest, Non-alignment, and African interests. However, International terrorism is not a principle that governs Nigeria's foreign policy. Instead, it is a global issue that Nigeria, like other countries, is working to combat.

explanation: It offended their pride at independence. The question is self explanatory in relation to the answer.

explanation: A propaganda is any information, especially of a biased or misleading nature, used to promote a political cause or point of view.

topics.title: PUBLIC OPINION

subject: Government
q: A political ideology that advocates extreme nationalism and suppression of individual liberties is
explanation: Fascism is a political ideology that advocates extreme nationalism and suppression of individual liberties. It is a form of far-right, authoritarian ultranationalism characterized by dictatorial power, forcible suppression of opposition, and strong regimentation of society and of the economy which came to prominence in early 20th-century Europe.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES

subject: Government
q: Maxism is directed against
options: state ownership of the means of production
explanation: Marxism is a social, political, and economic theory originated by Karl Marx, which focuses on the struggle between capitalists and the working class. Marx opposed the idea of capitalism, where the means of production are owned by private individuals. Therefore, Marxism is directed against state ownership of the means of production. The other options do not accurately represent the principles of Marxism.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : SOCIALISM

q: The President of Nigeria was indirectly elected through a secret ballot for a period of five years by the Senate in
explanation: The President of Nigeria was indirectly elected through a secret ballot for a period of five years by the Senate in 1979. This was under the 1979 constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. The other years do not correspond to the period when the President was elected in this manner.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : INDEPENDENCE CONSTITUTION 1960

q: The first franchise in the history of the democratic process is
explanation: Suffrage for men and women aged 18 granted in 1975. Universal adult male suffrage for those over 25 was introduced in 1925. Universal adult suffrage for both sexes over 20 introduced in 1946, ratified by the new Constitution which adopted on 3 May 1947.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

subject: Government
q: A vote taken by the citizenry on an important political issue is a
explanation: A vote taken by the citizenry on an important political issue is a referendum. A referendum is a direct and universal vote in which an entire electorate is invited to vote on a particular proposal or issue. This can lead to the adoption of a new law, constitution, or government policy. Therefore, the correct answer is a referendum.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POLITICAL PROCESSES: POLITICAL SOCIALIZATION, POLITICAL PARTICIPATION AND POLITICAL CULTURE

q: Before the O.A.U was formed in 1963, Nigeria was a member of the
explanation: The Monrovia Group, sometimes known as the Monrovia bloc, officially the Conference of Independent African States, was a short-lived, informal association of African states with a shared vision of the future of Africa and of Pan-Africanism in the early 1960s.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS : OAU/AU

subject: Government
q: In a monarchial form of Government, sovereignty resides with
explanation: In a monarchial form of Government, sovereignty resides with the royalty. A monarchy is a form of government in which a person, the monarch, is head of state for life or until abdication. The political legitimacy and authority of the monarch may vary from purely symbolic (crowned republic), to restricted (constitutional monarchy), to fully autocratic (absolute monarchy), and can expand across the domains of the executive, legislative and judicial.

topics.title: SYSTEMS OF GOVERNANCE : MONARCHICAL SYSTEM OF GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: A characteristic of a fascist state is the existence of a
explanation: A characteristic of a fascist state is the existence of a one-party system. Fascism is a form of far-right, authoritarian political ideology that centralizes power around a single party or leader. It suppresses opposition and criticism, and often uses a policy of belligerent nationalism and racism.

topics.title: POLITICAL IDEOLOGIES : FASCISM

subject: Government
q: The state legislatures of the second republic in Nigeria had the following "except" a
explanation: The state legislatures of the second republic in Nigeria had a speaker, a deputy speaker, and a clerk of the house, but not a senate leader. The senate leader is a position in the Senate, not in the state legislatures.

topics.title: INSTITUTIONS OF GOVERNMENT IN THE POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : THE LEGISLATIVE – STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND WORKING.

subject: Government
q: The process of law-making by the legislature begins with
explanation: The process of law-making by the legislature, also known as the legislative process, begins with the initiation of a bill. A bill is a proposal for a new law, or a proposal to change an existing law, presented for debate and approval. Once a bill is introduced, it undergoes several stages including debate, committee scrutiny, and readings before it is passed into law.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION

subject: Government
q: The process of removing on elected official by the electorate after an election is termed
explanation: The process of removing an elected official by the electorate after an election is termed 'recall'. Recall is a procedure that allows citizens to remove and replace a public official before the end of a term of office. It differs from impeachment, which is a formal process in which an official is accused of unlawful activity, and which may or may not lead to the removal of that official from office.

topics.title: ELECTIONS

subject: Government
q: A key characteristic of the civil service is
explanation: A key characteristic of the civil service is the merit system. The merit system is a method of hiring and promoting government employees based on their ability to perform a job, rather than on their political connections. It is designed to ensure fairness and eliminate patronage and nepotism.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: The Civil service cadre responsible for the general supervision and control of ministries is the
explanation: The Civil service cadre responsible for the general supervision and control of ministries is the administrative cadre. The administrative cadre is the topmost and most important cadre in the civil service. They are responsible for policy formulation, planning, and overall supervision of the implementation of policies.

topics.title: THE CIVIL SERVICE

subject: Government
q: Privatization and commercialization of public enterprises in Nigeria will lead to further entrenchment of
explanation: Privatization and commercialization of public enterprises in Nigeria will lead to further entrenchment of capitalism. Privatization is the process of transferring an enterprise or industry from the public sector to the private sector. The private sector follows the principles of capitalism where businesses are owned and operated by individuals and companies for profit. Commercialization is the process of managing or running something principally for financial gain, which is also a principle of capitalism.

q: Which of the following countries is closely associated with the development of Ajaokuta Steel Project?
explanation: The Ajaokuta Steel Project is closely associated with Russia. The Ajaokuta Steel Company, located in Ajaokuta, Kogi State, Nigeria, is considered to be the largest steel mill in Nigeria. It was started by the Soviet Union (now Russia) in 1979 under a cooperation agreement with Nigeria.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS : NIGERIA'S FOREIGN POLICY

q: When did the Federal Military Government abolish the four regions in Nigeria?
explanation: General T.J. Aguiyi-Ironsi abolished the regional confederated form of government and pursued unitary systems.

explanation: Major General Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi did this after the coup in 1966. Ironsi promulgated Decree Number 34 of 1966, which abrogated the federal system of government and substituted a unitary system.

explanation: The Nigerian Council of 1914 was created by Sir Frederick Lugard after the amalgamation of the southern and northern protectorates in 1914. The council consisted of 36 members to advise him on legislative matters.

subject: Government
q: The foremost British trading company on the West African coast was
explanation: The foremost British trading company on the West African coast was the Royal Niger Company. The Royal Niger Company was a mercantile company chartered by the British government in the nineteenth century. It was formed in 1879 as the United African Company and renamed to Royal Niger Company in 1886.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: The emergence of nationalism was essentially the result of the bills of
explanation: Nationalism is a political ideology that involves a strong identification of a group of individuals with a political entity defined in national terms, i.e. a nation. In the late 19th century, many people in Africa, Asia, and Latin America developed a new sense of national identity. This was largely a result of colonialism, where European powers took control of other countries. The people in these countries began to identify themselves as a nation in order to resist colonial rule. Therefore, the emergence of nationalism was essentially the result of colonialism.

topics.title: PROCESS OF DECOLONIZATION : NATIONALIST MOVEMENTS – EMERGENCE, GOALS, STRATEGIES

subject: Government
q: The political party that replaced NNDP before independence was
explanation: The Nigerian Youth Movement (NYM) was the political party that replaced the Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) before independence. The NNDP was the first political party in Nigeria, founded by Herbert Macaulay in 1923. However, it was replaced by the NYM in the 1930s. The NYM was a more nationalistic party, seeking to unite all Nigerians in the struggle for independence.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FIRST REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: A tactic employed by pressure groups to achieve their objectives is
explanation: Pressure groups often use lobbying as a tactic to achieve their objectives. Lobbying involves influencing or persuading a legislator to vote in favor of a particular policy or legislation that the pressure group supports. This is done through various means such as meetings, presentations, or providing research and evidence to support their cause. Therefore, the correct answer is 'lobbying'.

topics.title: PRESSURE GROUPS

subject: Government
q: Citizenship may be changed by
explanation: Citizenship can be changed by renunciation. Renunciation is the act of giving up one's citizenship voluntarily. This is usually done by making a formal declaration of renunciation to the appropriate government authority. Remarriage, divorce, and conviction do not necessarily lead to a change in citizenship. Therefore, the correct answer is 'renunciation'.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP

subject: Government
q: The citizenship of a country can be acquired by an alien through all the following except
explanation: Citizenship can be acquired by an alien through investment, marriage, and naturalization. Investment refers to the process where an individual invests a certain amount of money in the country and in return, they are granted citizenship. This is often referred to as citizenship by investment. Marriage is another common way to acquire citizenship, often referred to as citizenship by marriage. Naturalization is the legal process where a non-citizen in the country may acquire citizenship. However, tourism is not a means to acquire citizenship. Tourists are individuals who are visiting the country for a short period and they do not have the rights and privileges of a citizen.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Government
q: The motion of equality before the law is the same as the principle of
explanation: The motion of equality before the law is the same as the principle of rule of law. The rule of law is a principle under which all persons, institutions, and entities are accountable to laws that are: publicly promulgated, equally enforced, independently adjudicated, and consistent with international human rights principles. The principle of the rule of law is intended to be a safeguard against arbitrary governance. Supremacy of the constitution, independence of the judiciary, and social equality are all important concepts in a democratic society, but they are not the same as the principle of equality before the law.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

explanation: Judicial review is a process under which executive and legislative actions are subject to review by the judiciary.

topics.title: CONSTITUTION : TYPES OF CONSTITUTION

q: The central legislature of Nigeria became bicameral in
explanation: The central legislature of Nigeria became bicameral in 1960. McPherson constitution of 1951 created two chambers regional legislatures known as the house of chiefs and the house of Assembly in the Northern and Western region. Coming then to independence constitution of 1960. Bicameral legislatures were adopted in both the federal and regional government including the Eastern region.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : LEGISLATURE TYPES, STRUCTURE, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: The transfer of authority to local government councils is known as
explanation: The transfer of authority to local government councils is known as devolution. Devolution is the statutory delegation of powers from the central government of a sovereign state to govern at a subnational level, such as a regional or local level. It is a form of administrative decentralization.

explanation: Foreign missions have diplomatic immunity of their envoys. They are not subject to arrest or prosecution under local laws.

explanation: Nigeria broke diplomatic relation with France because France wanted to test an atomic bomb in the sahara part of Nigeria.

topics.title: ELECTIONS

subject: Government
q: The formation of confederations is based primarily on
explanation: The formation of confederations is primarily based on security considerations. A confederation is a union of sovereign states, united for purposes of common action often in relation to other states. The key feature of such a system is that the member states retain their sovereignty, and the union does not have the power to make decisions on behalf of the member states without their consent. The primary reason for forming such a union is often to enhance the security of the member states by presenting a united front against potential threats.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE

subject: Government
q: A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called
explanation: It is a system of government by the whole population or all the eligible members of a state, typically through elected representatives. It promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens.

subject: Government
q: One feature of public corporations that was weakened by privatization is
explanation: One feature of public corporations that was weakened by privatization is government control. Privatization is the process of transferring an enterprise or industry from the public sector to the private sector. The public sector is the part of the economic system that is run by government agencies. Privatization often involves the sale of public assets to private owners, which reduces the level of control that the government has over these assets.

subject: Government
q: In the 1930s, the political movement that challenged the NNDP domination of Lagos politics was the
explanation: In the 1930s, the political movement that challenged the NNDP (Nigerian National Democratic Party) domination of Lagos politics was the NYM (Nigerian Youth Movement). The NYM was a Nigerian nationalist organization that emerged in 1934 and became the most powerful political organization of its time. It was formed to promote nationalistic pride and to drive for independence.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY POLITICS IN POST-INDEPENDENCE NIGERIA : FIRST REPUBLIC

subject: Government
q: A typical form of delegated legislation is
explanation: A typical form of delegated legislation is 'bye-laws'. Delegated legislation refers to laws made by an individual or body under powers given to them by an Act of Parliament. It allows others to make law themselves, under powers given to them by an Act of Parliament. Bye-laws are laws made by a local authority or corporation using powers given to them by an Act of Parliament. They are local laws made by a local council to cover issues in their particular area.

topics.title: PROCESSES OF LEGISLATION : DELEGATED LEGISLATION

subject: Government
q: In a democratic system
explanation: In a democratic system, the principle of majority rule is fundamental. This means that the option or decision that receives the most votes is the one that is chosen. This is not to say that the rights of the minority are disregarded, but rather that the majority's decision is given precedence. The concept of 'majority vote' is therefore the most accurate answer.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: That type of sovereignty that thrives on legitimacy is
explanation: Popular or political sovereignty belongs to the people through which the government by the constitution obtains all its powers and authorities.

topics.title: BASIC CONCEPTS OF GOVERNMENT : POWER, AUTHORITY, LEGITIMACY AND SOVEREIGNTY

subject: Government
q: The right of a citizen to seek redress means the right to
explanation: Seek an appeal under another judgement is the best answer because the right to seek redress as a fundamental element of rule of law must mean that aggrieved parties should be afforded the opportunity to have their cases re-examined especially by a court of higher authority.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : CITIZENSHIP RIGHTS

subject: Government
q: An order compelling a lower court or public corporation to perform in accordance with legitimacy is called
explanation: Writ (i.e Order) of Mandamus is usually issued to mandate a public authority to perform its statutory or the functions tied to its establishment. On the alternative, the great writ means the habeas corpus writ issued to order the release of a body or person unlawfully detained or imprisoned to stand judgement.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: A form of government which has its slogan to be 'ruling of the best' is
explanation: Aristocracy being a rule by the Noble is believed to be a ruling of the best class of persons. Timocracy on the other hand, is believed by Aristotle to be a government strictly by property-owners with emphasis on honour and proprietary interest holdings.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT : ARISTOCRACY FEATURES, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: Deconcentration of power means the
options: sharing of powers among tiers of government
creation of semi autonomous units to govern other parts of a state
process of setting strategies or policies for the subsidiary territories in the state by putting recognized personnel in place
act of delegating powers and authority to component units that exist in a state
explanation: Deconcentration of power refers to the redistribution or dispersal of functions, powers, people or things away from a central location or authority. In the context of government, it means the creation of semi autonomous units to govern other parts of a state. This is done to ensure that power is not concentrated in one central authority, but is spread out to allow for more local control and participation.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE : UNITARY FEATURES, REASONS FOR ADOPTION, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: The concept of the independence of the judiciary means that
options: the judiciary must not attempt to meddle into legislative and executive affairs
the powers of the courts can not be subjected of executive re-assessment
the sanctity of the office of the Judges and Justices must be preserved without prejudice
all means of legislative or executive influence on judicial decision making must be disabled
explanation: Strictly speaking, independence of the judiciary isn't restricted to the idea of non-interference by the legislature or executive or either of agencies on judicial decision making but particularly ensuring that all the gateways to the activities that may put the judiciary in a vulnerable state to the other arms of government must be shut down.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : JUDICIARY FUNCTIONS, POWERS AND COMPONENTS

subject: Government
q: The political party serves as
options: A platform upon which the rights and liberties of the people can be attained
the group aggregating the interest of all in the state
a platform for rebelling against military rule
explanation: Political parties serve as a platform for interest representation. They aggregate the interests of their members and constituents and articulate these interests in the political arena. They play a crucial role in a democratic system as they provide a link between the citizens and the government. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a platform for interest representation'.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY SYSTEMS : POLITICAL PARTIES – DEFINITION, ORGANIZATION, FUNCTIONS.

q: A party can be said to have national outlook if
options: it is ethnic based but has branches all over the state
it is registered by the electoral commission as a national party
it is headed by individuals who are famous within and without the country
explanation: Unlike the ethnic based philosophy, nature or outlook of the Action Group, Northern Peoples Congress in the First Republic, a multi-ethnic and all embracing political party is that with a Nation wide outlook.

topics.title: POLITICAL PARTIES AND PARTY SYSTEMS : POLITICAL PARTIES – DEFINITION, ORGANIZATION, FUNCTIONS.

explanation: At the second stage or reading of a bill, the debate on the subject matter of concern of the bill begins, this process becomes heightened at the Third Stage and particularly at the Committee Stage.

options: the same as impeachment in a presidential system
the ability of the head of state to dissolve the parliament when collective responsibility fails to hold
the act of showing disapproval to the prime minister having breached the collective responsibility doctrine
the disapproval with dismissal in effect, the ministers and its head
explanation: The implication of the passage of a vote of no confidence is to demonstrate disapproval which will lead to the retirement of the entire cabinet and its head.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: A cabinet re-shuffle takes place......
options: only when a motion of no confidence is passed
when prorogation and collective responsibility have their way
when the shadow cabinet fails in its effectiveness
explanation: The membership of the Cabinet will be substituted or reshuffled especially there is dissolution of the cabinet in its entirely in a WestMinster system.

topics.title: ARMS OF GOVERNMENT : EXECUTIVE-TYPES, FUNCTIONS AND POWERS

subject: Government
q: Popular political participation means
options: having majority of the people partaking in politics
having a good number of voters in the electoral process
the aggregate efforts of more than half of the citizens to partake in public debate and politics
the sum total of all activities exerted by the majority with enthusiasm actively involving in all aspects of their political sphere
explanation: The sum total of all activities exerted by the majority with enthusiasm actively involving in all aspects of their political sphere is the best answer not only because is the all encompassing answer but also because popular political participation is the aggegration of all efforts of the citizens partaking delibrately and consciously in their governance affairs.

q: Citizenship by naturalization
options: is the same as citizenship by marriage and registeration
refers to the process of attaining citizenship through honorary means
is the act of a non-citizen having satisfied the constitutional criteria becoming a legal member of a state
is the medium by which an alien through the judiciary receives certification and attains citizenship by birth
explanation: Citizenship by naturalization provides the medium for non-citizens who have satisfied or fulfilled the requirements of good citizenship according to a State Law, to become Citizens.

topics.title: CITIZENSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

q: The last measure used by the pressure groups issuing a condition to be satisfied within a stipulated time is called
explanation: Pressure groups commonly issued deadlines(ultimatum) for the government to fulfil their requests and queries before embarking on strike action.

explanation: Elitist is often used as a critique label, to describe a political party definitive of social class and rankings of top profiled politicians without much agenda for social justice.

explanation: A military junta is a term sufficient to define an assembly of rulership organised by a council of militatry officers especially after a successful coup.

topics.title: FORMS OF GOVERNMENT

subject: Government
q: The non-stratified society in the pre-colonial era was that of the ........ where decisions were made
explanation: The Igbo precolonial was seen predominantly as acephalous and decisions were made based on popular consultations and acceptability.

topics.title: PRE-COLONIAL POLITICS : HAUSA PRE-COLONIAL SYSTEM

subject: Government
q: The Hausa/Fulani Empire as a result of its heterogeneous population adopted
options: A unitary system where full concentration of power prevailed
A federal structure where powers were decentralized although autocratic
A partial federal set up with deconcentration
explanation: The Hausa/Fulani Empire, due to its heterogeneous population, adopted a quasi-federal structure with devolution. This means that while the central authority (the Sultan) had the ultimate power, some powers were devolved to the local authorities (the Emirs). This is different from a unitary system where all powers are concentrated at the central level, or a federal system where powers are divided between the central and local levels. A quasi-federal structure is a compromise between these two extremes, allowing for some level of local autonomy while maintaining central control.

topics.title: STRUCTURES OF GOVERNANCE : FEDERAL FEATURES, REASONS FOR ADOPTION, MERITS AND DEMERITS

subject: Government
q: Twelve States creation is a reknown achievement of the ... regime
explanation: The creation of twelve states in Nigeria is an achievement of the Yakubu Gowon's regime. On May 27, 1976, General Yakubu Gowon, the then military head of state, announced the creation of twelve states in Nigeria, replacing the four regions that existed at the time. This was done as a strategy to keep Nigeria united during the civil war.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : OLIVER LYTTLETON CONSTITUTION 1954

subject: Government
q: The basis of the Nigerian foreign policy thrust particularly during the Independence Constitution era was ... in nature
explanation: Afrocentrism is the term used to describe the nature and interests of the Nigerian foreign policy targets withy preference for African neighboring States.

topics.title: NIGERIA’S FOREIGN POLICY : NIGERIA’S NON-ALIGNMENT POLICY

subject: Government
q: The Brussel conference in 1889/90 was based on
options: furthering the decision on colonialism as previously discussed in the Berlin conference
deciding on how to penetrate Africa for economic adventurism
explanation: The Brussels Conference of 1889-90 was a follow-up to the Berlin Conference of 1884-85. The main aim of the Brussels Conference was to further the decisions made at the Berlin Conference, which primarily dealt with the 'Scramble for Africa' and the rules for colonial acquisition. However, the Brussels Conference focused more on the suppression of the slave trade and improving the lives of the native people. Therefore, the closest answer is 'furthering the decision on colonialism as previously discussed in the Berlin conference'.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : THE BRITISH PROCESS OF ACQUISITION – TRADE, MISSIONARY ACTIVITIES, COMPANY RULE, CROWN COLONY, PROTECTORATE

subject: Government
q: According to Lewis Samuel, 'is identified with pure conquest and unequivocal exploitation...'
explanation: The quote is referring to colonialism. Colonialism is the policy or practice of acquiring full or partial political control over another country, occupying it with settlers, and exploiting it economically. Lewis Samuel's quote describes colonialism as a system of pure conquest and unequivocal exploitation, which is the essence of colonialism.

topics.title: IMPERIALIST PENETRATION : IMPACT OF BRITISH COLONIAL RULE- ECONOMIC, POLITICAL, SOCIO-CULTURAL

the council could only make law for the southern protectorate
the executive council was powerful than it

subject: Government
q: An important provision of the 1946 constitution was that it ........
options: introduced official majority in the legislative council for the 1st time
for the 1st time, introduced the office of the head of government at the regional level
explanation: The 1946 constitution, also known as the Richard's constitution, introduced official majority in the legislative council for the first time. This means that the government officials had the majority of the seats in the legislative council. This was done to ensure that the government had control over the legislative process. However, this was criticized as it undermined the principle of representative democracy.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : ARTHUR RICHARDS CONSTITUTION 1946

subject: Government
q: In the all-nigerian constitutional conference of 1950
options: all official members of the legislative council participated
it was resolved that the Nigerians federation be organized on ethnic basis
it was concluded that a quasi-federal structure should be developed for colonial Nigeria
all unofficial members of the legislative council participated
explanation: The all-Nigerian constitutional conference of 1950 concluded that a quasi-federal structure should be developed for colonial Nigeria. This was to allow for a degree of self-governance at the regional level while maintaining a strong central government. This structure was seen as a compromise between the desire for self-governance and the need for a unified Nigeria.

topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : JOHN MACPHERSON CONSTITUTION 1951

subject: Government
q: Implementations of the target programmes of the government is the primary duty of the ... a part of the
explanation: The primary duty of implementing the target programmes of the government falls under the civil Service, which is a part of the public Service. The Civil Service is a sector of government composed mainly of career bureaucrats hired on professional merit rather than appointed or elected, whose institutional tenure typically survives transitions of political leadership. The Public Service, on the other hand, is a broader term that includes the Civil Service and other individuals or bodies that work for the state.

explanation: The 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are the most recent aspirational global agenda for the sake of environmental sustainability, strong governance institutions and partnerships among related others.

explanation: Under the 1963 constitution, the federal judge could be removed by the Federal Judicial Service Commission. The Federal Judicial Service Commission is a body responsible for the appointment, promotion, and disciplinary control of judicial officers of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. It is part of the judicial arm of government and plays a crucial role in the administration of justice in the country.

The General Assembly is one of the six main organs of the U.N. and is the only one in which all member nations have equal representation. It is responsible for making decisions on important questions, such as those on peace and security, admission of new members and budgetary matters, and it can make recommendations to the United Nations system on international issues within its competence.

It is used to aggregate popular thinking and standpoint on a subject matter, to evaluate the political strength of a political party, and to inspire public reasoning on population census. However, it is not used for preparing public expenditure. Public expenditure is prepared based on the budget and financial resources of the government, not on public opinion.

The policy involved using traditional rulers and structures to govern on behalf of the British.

explanation: The cultural organisation which metamorphosed into the Northern People's Congress (NPC) was Jamiyyar Mutanem Arewa. explanation: Citizenship rights are the legal privileges and opportunities available to the citizens of a State. subject: Government
q: A moral or legal conviction for the observance of the constitution may be described as
explanation: The question refers to a moral or legal conviction for the observance of the constitution. This is best described as 'constitutionalism'. Constitutionalism is the idea that government should be limited in its powers and that its authority depends on its observation of these limitations. The rule of law is a principle under which all persons, institutions, and entities are accountable to laws, but it doesn't necessarily imply a moral or legal conviction for the observance of the constitution. Immunity refers to being exempt from certain legal obligations or liabilities, and political obligation refers to the moral duty of citizens to obey the laws of their state.

explanation: Gerrymandering is the process of creating political constituencies in order to favour the electoral scores of a political party.

explanation: The Chick's commission was set up in 1951 to look into the issue of revenue allocation in Nigeria. The commission was established by the British colonial government to review the revenue allocation formula and principles in operation at the time and to recommend what changes were needed. The commission's recommendations led to a significant change in the revenue allocation formula, which had a profound impact on the fiscal relations between the federal and regional governments.

q: Under the 1979 constitution of Nigeria, each State was allowed to elect
options: from two to five senators depending on the population

explanation: Under the 1979 constitution of Nigeria, each state was allowed to elect three senators. This was done to ensure equal representation of all states in the Senate, regardless of their population size. This system is known as equal representation, and it is used in many federal systems around the world to ensure that all regions of the country have an equal voice in the upper house of the legislature.

explanation: The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is one of the six main organs of the United Nations. It has 15 members, and each member has one vote. explanation: A written constitution provides for amendment procedures, the powers and limitations of the government, and the fundamental rights of the citizens. q: The fundamental problem facing Nigeria federalism is
explanation: The fundamental problem facing Nigeria federalism is revenue allocation. Federalism is a system of government where power is divided between a central authority and constituent political units. In Nigeria, the issue of revenue allocation has been a major problem as different states and regions believe they are not getting a fair share of the national revenue. This has led to conflicts and demands for resource control in various parts of the country.

q: The factors influencing Nigeria's foreign policy include all except
explanation: The factors influencing Nigeria's foreign policy include national interest, defence, and economic resources. However, geographical society is not a factor influencing Nigeria's foreign policy. Foreign policy is influenced by factors such as national interest which refers to the goals or objectives of a nation, defence which refers to the measures taken by a nation to ensure its security, and economic resources which refers to the wealth of a nation in terms of its production of goods and services. Geographical society does not directly influence a nation's foreign policy.

q: The major changes introduced bt the Clifford constitution include all except
options: The constitution isolated the Northern provinces in Nigeria
It establishes the elective principle for the first time
explanation: The Clifford Constitution of 1922 was the first constitution of British Nigeria that introduced the elective principle. However, it did not abolish the Nigerian Council. The Nigerian Council was a legislative body established by the British colonial authorities in Nigeria. The council was made up of the Governor as the President, 4 ex-officio members, 3 nominated official members, 15 nominated unofficial members and 3 elected members. The Clifford Constitution rather replaced the Nigerian Council with a new legislative council.

explanation:
The features of Republican constitution of 1963 are:

- The Queen of England ceased to be the head of state of Nigeria

explanation: The control of local government does not include 'Press control'. The local government is controlled by various means such as federal control, audit control, and pressure control. Federal control refers to the power that the federal government has over local governments. Audit control refers to the checks and balances in place to ensure that local governments are using their funds appropriately. Pressure control refers to the influence that citizens and interest groups can exert on local government. Press control, however, is not a form of control over local government. The press can influence public opinion and hold governments accountable, but it does not control them.

the sum total of all activities exerted by the majority with enthusiasm actively involving in all aspects of their political sphere
explanation: The sum total of all activities exerted by the majority with enthusiasm actively involving in all aspects of their political sphere is the best answer not only because is the all encompassing answer but also because popular political participation is the aggegration of all efforts of the citizens partaking delibrately and consciously in their governance affairs.

Its powers include the establishment of peacekeeping operations, the establishment of international sanctions, and the authorization of military action. The UNSC comprises of 15 members. explanation: The control of local government includes federal control, audit control, and pressure control. Federal control refers to the power that the central government has over local governments. Audit control refers to the financial oversight that is conducted to ensure that local governments are using funds appropriately. Pressure control refers to the influence that citizens and interest groups can exert on local government. However, press control is not a form of local government control. The press is an independent entity that is meant to hold government accountable, not to control it.

subject: Government
q: The features of federalism include all except
explanation: Federalism is a system of government where power is divided between a central government and individual states or provinces. Each level of government has its own sovereignty (in areas the constitution allows) and can make decisions independently of the other levels. Therefore, the supremacy of the central government is not a feature of federalism. Instead, there is a balance of power. The other options such as supremacy of the constitution, the constitution being rigid, and encouraging succession are features of federalism. The constitution is supreme in that it outlines the powers of each level of government, it is rigid in that it is not easily changed, and federalism can encourage succession in that it allows for a degree of self-governance at the state or provincial level.

explanation: The Lyttleton Constitution of 1954 is considered the constitution that gave Nigeria a true structure of federalism. This constitution introduced federalism in real terms by decentralizing the system and allowing regions to have their own constitution, judiciary, and public service. The other options do not accurately represent the constitution that established federalism in Nigeria.

explanation: The factors influencing Nigeria's foreign policy include national interest, defense, and economic resources. Foreign policy is influenced by factors such as national interest, which refers to the goals or objectives of a nation that are beneficial to its citizens; defense, which refers to the measures taken to protect the nation's sovereignty; and economic resources, which refers to the nation's wealth in terms of goods, services, or materials. 'Geographical society' does not fit into these categories.

subject: Government
q: Maxism is directed against
options: state ownership of the means of production
explanation: Marxism is a social, political, and economic theory originated by Karl Marx, which focuses on the struggle between capitalists and the working class. Marx argued that the capitalist bourgeoisie, an economic minority, maintains control over society and exploits the proletariat, the working class majority. However, Marxism is not directed against the proletariat or socialism. Instead, it advocates for the proletariat and socialism. It is also not against materialism, as Marx's theory is a form of historical materialism. The statement that Marxism is directed against 'state ownership of the means of production' is the most accurate among the options, as Marx advocated for the abolition of private property and the establishment of state ownership of the means of production.

q: Unitary system of government is best applicable to a/an
explanation: A unitary system of government is a system of political organization with a central supreme government which holds the authority over and makes the decisions for subordinate local governments. This system is best applicable to a homogeneous state where the people share similar culture, language, and belief. This is because it is easier to implement policies and decisions in such a state as there is less likelihood of resistance due to cultural or regional differences.

explanation: The Westminster system of government is a democratic parliamentary system of government modelled after the politics of the United Kingdom. topics.title: CONSTITUTIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN NIGERIA : INDEPENDENCE CONSTITUTION 1960

This is similar to the federal system of government where each state has its own government and operates independently of the federal government.

explanation: The Aare- ona kankanfo is the head of the army in the Yoruba pre-colonial system; the equivalent is the Madawaki who is the head of the army in the Hausa/Fulani pre-colonial system. The Waziri is the prime minister; the Galadima is head of the capital while the Dogari is the head of the police.

explanation: The loi-cadre ( Reform Act) was a French legal reform passed by the French National Assembly on 23 June 1956. It marked a turning point in relations between France and its overseas empire. Under pressure from independence movements in the colonies, the government transferred a number of powers from Paris to elected territorial governments in French African colonies and also removed remaining voting inequalities by implementing universal suffrage and abolishing the multiple electoral college system.

Exclusive Legislative List
